findings.context: Local runtime env used stale fallback project `sentra-healthcare-solution` instead of the active project. Remediated in-place.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep `GCP_PROJECT_ID` set to `abyss-monorepo` in the local `.env` and re-check any local shells or process managers that may cache old values.

findings.context: Example env left `GCP_PROJECT_ID` blank, which made new setups prone to drift or fallback to outdated project values. Remediated in repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep the example pinned to `abyss-monorepo` unless the team intentionally changes the canonical active project.

findings.context: Package example env hardcoded `GOOGLE_PROJECT_ID=sentra-healthcare-solution`, which would route Vertex fallback traffic to the wrong project. Remediated in repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep `GOOGLE_PROJECT_ID=abyss-monorepo` in this package example and align onboarding docs to the same value.

findings.context: Package example env used generic placeholder `your-project-id`, which was not migration-safe for this monorepo's canonical GCP project. Remediated in repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep the example pinned to `abyss-monorepo` for consistent local setup and smoke testing.

findings.context: Runtime connector fell back to `sentra-healthcare-solution` when env was missing, creating a silent wrong-project risk for Vertex RAG calls. Remediated to resolve project ID from env only.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep `resolveProjectId()` and avoid reintroducing hardcoded project fallbacks in runtime connectors.

findings.context: Internal Vertex engine fell back to `sentra-healthcare-solution`, which could misroute generation and retrieval requests. Remediated to require configured env-based project resolution.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep project resolution centralized and fail fast when no valid project env is present.

findings.context: Discovery Engine initialization script hardcoded fallback `sentra-healthcare-solution`, which could create resources in the wrong project. Remediated in repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Run this script only with `GCP_PROJECT_ID`, `GOOGLE_PROJECT_ID`, or `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` explicitly set to `abyss-monorepo`.

findings.context: Public data ingestion script hardcoded fallback `sentra-healthcare-solution`, which could send ingestion-side setup steps to the wrong project. Remediated in repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep env-driven project resolution and validate shell env before running ingestion workflows.

findings.context: Discovery Engine connector hardcoded project number `382444138233` instead of the active canonical project ID, creating a hidden routing mismatch. Remediated in repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep the connector on env-driven project resolution and avoid project-number literals unless they are derived from deployment config.

findings.context: Workflow reads `vars.GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`; the YAML is fine, but effective runtime project depends on an external GitHub repository variable not stored in this repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Verify the repository or organization variable `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` is set to `abyss-monorepo` and that `SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` / `GCP_WIF_PROVIDER` point to the same project lineage.

findings.context: Workflow reads `vars.GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`; the YAML is fine, but effective runtime project depends on an external GitHub repository variable not stored in this repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Verify the repository or organization variable `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` is set to `abyss-monorepo` and that `SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` / `GCP_WIF_PROVIDER` point to the same project lineage.

findings.context: Workflow reads `vars.GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`; the YAML is fine, but effective runtime project depends on an external GitHub repository variable not stored in this repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Verify the repository or organization variable `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` is set to `abyss-monorepo` and that `SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` / `GCP_WIF_PROVIDER` point to the same project lineage.

findings.context: Workflow reads `vars.GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`; the YAML is fine, but effective runtime project depends on an external GitHub repository variable not stored in this repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Verify the repository or organization variable `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` is set to `abyss-monorepo` and that `SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` / `GCP_WIF_PROVIDER` point to the same project lineage.

findings.context: Workflow reads `vars.GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`; the YAML is fine, but effective runtime project depends on an external GitHub repository variable not stored in this repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Verify the repository or organization variable `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` is set to `abyss-monorepo` and that `SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` / `GCP_WIF_PROVIDER` point to the same project lineage.

migration_checklist: Confirm there are zero remaining repo matches for `named-indexer-494305-c8`, `sentra-healthcare-solution`, `382444138233`, and `your-project-id` on active monorepo surfaces.
Require runtime scripts and connectors to resolve project ID from env (`GCP_PROJECT_ID`, `GOOGLE_PROJECT_ID`, or `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`) and fail fast when unset.
Verify GitHub repository or organization variables `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`, `SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL`, and `GCP_WIF_PROVIDER` all belong to `abyss-monorepo`.
Verify any service account emails, WIF providers, Secret Manager references, Artifact Registry paths, and deployment commands outside the repo no longer reference deleted project lineage.
Run targeted smoke checks for Vertex RAG / Discovery Engine flows with the new project in effect before the next deployment.
